2021/6/21 21.67 FM AsR Mean Yiwei liu (刘伊玮)

Tianjin Summer Solstice 2021, Final (2021天津夏至未至魔方赛, 决赛)

#1

http://fewestmov.es/if/a7008bc1bae2253e001144b780adaecb

R' U' F D' B' D' L D2 F2 D' B2 D' B2 L2 B R D' R L' U2 F R' U' F

U' R2 F' U // EO (4/4)

B' L' // DR-2E3C (2/6)

U2 * B2 D2 F2 L' B' L // DR (7/13)

F D2 F L2 F' L2 B2 F // 2E2E (8/21)

*: L2 D2 R2 F2 U2 R2 D2 B2 (8-4)

Solution: U' R2 F' U B' L' U2 L2 D2 R2 F2 U2 R2 F2 L' B' L F D2 F L2 F' L2 B2 F (25)

Optimal: R D2 F' U B L F L' F L2 B2 D2 B2 U' L D2 R2 L2 (18*)

#2

R' U' F U F B' L' B' U2 D' B U R' D2 L B2 D2 R' U2 L D2 U' R' U' F

(U2 F' R) // (3/3)

(D' L2 B' U B2) // P223 (5/8)

(L2 U' F2 U) // (4/12)

(F U' F' U F') // (5/17)

(D' B D' B' D2 B) // (6/23)

or represented as

(U2 F' R D') // P222 (4/4)

(L2 B' U B2) B' // 223 (5/9)

(L2 U' F2 U) // 223+sq (4/13)

(F U' F' U F') // white F2L-1 (5/18)

(D' B D' B' D2) // finish (5/23)

Solution: B' D2 B D B' D F U' F U F' U' F2 U L2 B2 U' B L2 D R' F U2 (23)

Optimal:

(U2 F' R) // EO (3/3)

(U' F D' B D' B') // DR (6/9)

(L2 U' F2 B2 D F2 D) // finish (7/16)

Optimal: D' F2 D' F2 B2 U L2 B D B' D F' U R' F U2 (16*)

#3 https://fewestmov.es/if/838d56a23da7757bf0dce1639c1dd681

R' U' F R B2 D2 B2 U2 F2 L' D2 F B2 U2 L' B2 R' B U D F U R' U' F

(R2 D F2 D2 U L') // DR (6/9)

D R2 D' * R2 D' // HTR (5/14)

L2 F2 U2 // 3E (3/17)

 

*: D R2 D' U F2 U' (6-6/17)

 

Solution: U F2 U' R2 D' L2 F2 U2 L U' D2 F2 D' R2 B' U B2 (17)

Optimal by Chong Wen:

F' U2 B U// questionable EO

(U2 L' D2 F' B' D2 L) // DR

(U2 B2 D2 L2 B) // finish

Optimal: F' U2 B U B' L2 D2 B2 U2 L' D2 F B D2 L U2 (16*)

2020/04/25 18.67 FM WB mean Tommy Kiprillis

on Tnoodle scramble with friends

#1. R' U' F L2 D B2 F2 D' R2 D B2 F2 L' R2 F D' R' B' F' L D' B2 D R' U' F

L B L // EO

(B' R2 D R2 F2 R2 D2 B') // DR (11)

B2 D R2 // lolwhat

D' U L2 U // 18 to 3c

U' L2 U' R2 U L2 U' R2 U2 // Finish (21)

Solution: L B L B2 D R2 D' R2 U L2 U' R2 U2 B D2 R2 F2 R2 D' R2 (21)

#2. R' U' F U2 B2 D2 L R' D2 L B2 L2 F D' R' U2 R2 F' L' R U' F' D' R' U' F

17

(L2 D R2 F) // EO + 222

L2 U2 F2 L2 U' L' // DR (10)

(U L2 F2 U' F2 L2) // almost block comm

(L U L' F2 L U' L') // WHAT THE FUCK LMAO (17)

Solution: L2 U2 F2 L F2 L U' L F2 U F2 L2 U' F' R2 D' L2 (17)

#3. R' U' F D' F2 U B2 R2 B2 F2 U B' F U2 F L B2 D F' R' B2 R D R' U' F

(L D') // EO

L U2 R F R' // DR (7)

D2 L2 F' U2 // 222

B' L2 B2 // 14 to 3c + e slice

L U2 R F R' D2 L2 F' ^ U2 B' $ L2 B2 # D L'

^ - F Fw'

# - B F' z // 15 to 3c

$ - B' R2 B L2 B' R2 B L2 (18)

Solution: L U2 R F R' D2 L2 Fw' U2 B2 R2 B L2 B' R2 F' z D L' (18)

2020/04/20 21.00 FM NB mean Baiqiang Dong (董百强)

​最少步QQ群 (.fm 卧槽 随便什么魔方)

#1 Scramble: R' U' F D R2 F2 D R2 B2 R2 B2 D2 B' D2 B L' U R B U2 F L2 U2 R' U' F

Solution: B U' F2 U2 F' U L' D' R D R D B2 D B2 D B2 L2 U R2 D F2 U' (23)

B U' F2 U2 F' // EO

U L' D' R D R // DR

NISS

U F2 D' R2 U' L2 // 223

B2 D' B2 D' B2 D' // finish

#2 Scramble: R' U' F U2 L D2 U2 L2 F2 R2 B2 U2 R2 U' B R2 F L D B' L2 B' R' D2 R' U' F

Solution: D2 R2 D F2 D' R' L U2 B2 L' U R' U2 D' R2 U R L2 B2 R' L U' B R' (24)

NISS

R B' // EO

U' L * // DR-2E3C

U' R2 U2 D R U' R $ // DR

D2 B2 D F2 D' R2 D2 // L5E

*: M U2 M' U2

$: M' D2 B2 M B2 D2

#3 Scramble: R' U' F D2 B2 L2 B2 D' B2 L2 D L2 U' F R F' U B' F' U' B2 L' R2 B R' U' F

Solution: D' F R U' D L U F2 U' B2 U' D' B D F' R' (16*)

D' F (R F D' B') // EO

R U' D L // DR

U F2 U' B2 U' D' // finish

21 single 25.00 NR mean Dylan Wang

Scramble 1: R' U' F L2 B2 F2 R2 F2 D U' B F2 L2 B' L2 R' F U2 L B2 R2 F2 R' U' F

Solution: F' L2 F2 U' F' U F L F' L F L2 F' L U2 L R' U' B R B (21)

(B' R' B' U R) // 223 (5)

(L' U2) // F2L-1 (7)

F' L2 F2 U' F' U // F2L (13)

F L F' L F L2 F' L // sune + pll skip (21)

Comments: There's another way to finish F2L that makes it a 20 move solution, but I feel that there was no way I would have seen it so I'm happy with 21. This scramble was so stupid, all you have to do is know NISS and find the right 2x2x2 and you're guaranteed 21. Still tied with Kevin Matthews for NR single because we broke it at the same time.

Scramble 2: R' U' F R2 U2 F2 D L2 R2 D' L2 U2 R F2 L2 R F' U F2 R B L B2 D2 R' U' F

Solution: D2 U' B R' U R' L U' R' U L' U' R2 U R' U R U' R L' B R' L U L2 D L2 F R' (29)

D2 U' B R' // 2 squares + pair (4)

(U F' L2 D' L2) // most of f2l (9)

(U' L' R B' L R') // EO (15)

(U R' U' R U' R' U * R U') // 3c left (24)

// * Inserted 3c (8-3) optimal

Comments: Everything after the 9th move felt stupid but it was still the best thing I found. Besides this, the best I found were 15 to 3e5c, and 21 to 5c. Doing L (F' D' R/R') to start solves EO in 4 moves but I found nothing after.

Scramble 3: R' U' F D2 F' L2 F D2 B' F2 L2 D L2 B2 F2 R B2 D L' B' L' U R' U' F

Solution: F U L U2 B' R2 D R B D B2 D L' D2 L R D R' D' L R D' R' D U2 (25)

(U2 L') // square (2)

F U L U2 // 222 (6)

B' R2 D R // 223 (10)

B // EO + pair (11)

D B2 D L' D2 L // F2L-1 (17)

R D R' D' L R D' R' D L' // OLS + LL Skip (27)

// 2 moves cancel on the skeleton (25)

Comments: 6 move 222 is bad but the 1 move EO saved it, and I only NISSed that square because I didn't realize the edge was flipped. I tried 3 different continuations after EO that gave 22 to 4c (30 finish), 22 to 5c (33 finish), and nothing. I then found a 19 to 3c3c (30 finish) on a different start.

I needed a 32 on this solve to tie NR mean, and NR felt guaranteed since I had 40 mins left to insert. I went back to the original start and tried more stuff which is when I found the lucky LL skip :)

The 21 carried the mean but I'm also glad my last 2 solves still averaged under the old NR mean (27.33)

2020/1/19 21.00 moves FM WR mean Cale Schoon

North Star Cubing Challenge 2020

#1 23 moves

R' U' F D' B2 R2 D U2 R2 U B2 R' U' B2 F' L' F2 U' B2 F2 R' B2 U' R' U' F

L2 F // EO

D R2 B2 D' R' // Domino in 7

F2 // pairs

B2 R2 // sq + more pairs

D // pseudo 223

(B2 D' F2) // 223 with inserted B2

(U2) // 15 to 3e3c + E slice

L2 F D R2 B2 D' R'

@ F2 B2 R2 D U2 F2 D B2

@ = R2 U' ^ R2 // to J perm + E slice

^ = U L2 F2 U F2 U' F2 D F2 D' L2 // J perm

L2 F D R2 B2 D' R

L2 F2 U F2 U' F2 D F2 D' [L2 R2 F2 B2 R2] D U2 F2 D B2

Replace brackets with F2 B2 L2

L2 F D R2 B2 D' R

L2 F2 U F2 U' F2 D F2 D' ^ F2 B2 L2 D U2 @ F2 D B2

@ = E

^ = E' // solve E slice

Solution: L2 F D R2 B2 D' R L2 F2 U F2 U' F2 D F2 U' R2 L2 F2 U' F2 D B2 (23)

Comments: easy 7 move domino is pretty lucky, but since corners were bad and there was only one pair I had to try lots of things just to get a 16 move finish. Optimal finish is 14 (21 final) which seems straightforward on inverse when looking at it, but since there were so many things to try I didn't find it despite using pretty much the entire attempt on this domino start. Other than that, I'm glad I was able to get within 2 moves of optimal finish on a domino start with bad corners, and the solution is pretty cool.

#2 18 moves

R' U' F U2 B D2 B F2 R2 F D2 B D' B U F2 D' R' F2 U2 R' F' R' U' F

F D' // sq

(F' D B F2 L') // 223

B U' B // EO

(U' B2) // sq

(L U L' U B2 U) // wth finish

Solution: F D' B U' B U' B2 U' L U' L' B2 U L F2 B' D' F (18)

Comments: overall pb single lol. Since none of the EOs really stood out I started by trying the 2 move square which had a lot of different options, and 16 minutes into the attempt I found the insane finish. Even though the solution doesn't use any crazy techniques, I still think it's cool that it switches scrambles 3 times and you have to do the right style of EO on the right scramble to find it.

The solution is optimal :D

#3 22 moves

R' U' F R2 U2 B' D2 L2 B L2 U2 B' D' F' L' B F2 D L R B2 F2 R' U' F

L' B R' U F' // EO

(D2 F2 L2 U' L') // Domino

(D' R2 U2 D F2 U' D2) // 17 to 3e

L' B R' U F' D2 U F2 D' U2 R2 D @ L U L2 F2 D2

@ = D2 B F' R2 F B'

Solution: L' B R' U F' D2 U F2 D' U2 R2 D' B F' R2 F B' L U L2 F2 D2 (22)

Comments: After 17 minutes I found a 23 off a different DR (same EO but continue on normal) which guaranteed me WR mean. Then I tried switching after EO and found this 10 move DR with crazy corners and blocks that easily lead to 15 to 3e + E slice. Optimal finish after domino is one move better but it doesn't seem like something I should have found.

FMC WR mean has been a huge goal of mine and I'm very happy with this :D

22.00 moves FMC WR Avg Sebastiano Tronto

at FMC 2019

#1 16 moves WR

N = R' U' F D2 L2 F R2 U2 R2 B D2 L B2 D' B2 L' R' B D2 B U2 L U2 R' U' F

I = F' U R U2 L' U2 B' D2 B' R L B2 D B2 L' D2 B' R2 U2 R2 F' L2 D2 F' U R

(U D' F R) // EO (4/4)

(L2 F' B2 U' B2 U' *) // DR (6/10)

(R2 @ B F D2) // 5e (4/14)

* = D' U F2 D U' R2 // 2e2e (6-4/16)

@ = E2 // 4x (x = "centers") (2/18)

D2 F' D2 U2 F' L2 D R2 D B2 F L2 R' F' D U' (16)

打乱：

R' U' F D2 L2 F R2 U2 R2 B D2 L B2 D' B2 L' R' B D2 B U2 L U2 R' U' F

逆打乱：

F' U R U2 L' U2 B' D2 B' R L B2 D B2 L' D2 B' R2 U2 R2 F' L2 D2 F' U R

首先复原LR轴向的EO（棱块色向）。

步骤：U D' F R

然后复原蓝绿块的色向，完成DR（Domino Reduction）。

步骤：L2 F' B2 U' B2 U'

值得一提的是，年初Harry Savage的单次17步解法也用了DR，关于DR的科普和分析见此

然后xjb操作一通，竟意外复原了所有角块！

步骤：R2 B F D2

此时仅剩下五个棱块未复原，于是整理步骤，进行后续的插入。

构造1：U D' F R L2 F' B2 U' B2 U' ① R2 B F D2

在①处插入三棱换公式：U D' F2 D U' R2

6步消去2步

此时剩下四个棱块，共16步。

构造2：U D' F R L2 F' B2 U' B2 D' F2 D U' B ② F D2

在②处进行一个骚操作：E2 （即Uw2 U2）

在复原四个棱块的同时，对换了前后左右的中心块。

此时的构造剩下四心换，共18步。

构造3：U D' F R L2 F' B2 U' B2 D' F2 D U' ③ B E2 F D2

在③处插入对心换公式：E M2 E' M2 （即Uw' U Rw2 R2 Uw U' Rw2 R2）

8步消去6步！

此时已经完成了一个20步的解法。

整理解法，将中层操作转写成标准步骤：

U D' F R L2 F' B2 U' B2 D' B2 D' U R2 L2 F U2 D2 F D2

将解法逆序，完成第一个20步的解法：

D2 F' D2 U2 F' L2 R2 U' D B2 D B2 U B2 F L2 R' F' D U'

观察一下这个解法，发现有个地方有些可疑：

D2 F' D2 U2 F' L2 R2 U' D B2 D B2 U B2 F L2 R' F' D U'

把这六步单独拎出来看看，发现可以用更短的步骤 R2 D R2 D来替换。

替换之后……等等，好像第一步R2又和原解法抵消了！

于是，世界上第一个官方16步的解法诞生了：

D2 F' D2 U2 F' L2 D R2 D B2 F L2 R' F' D U'

https://mp.weixin.qq.com/s?__biz=MzUzNDI5OTI1NA==&mid=2247488288&idx=1&sn=bea2fd8a11fc0164a651dc97049a17d1&chksm=fa978439cde00d2f95b3cd7fcf6916836aa2c3feb80dbbbfe1a3a384de75914429ba26f57e74&scene=21#wechat_redirect

* F' R' B' R F R' B R // 8-3

Solution // L2 F2 L B2 R' D R R D R' B2 U2 F2 R' B' R F R' B F U' D R' B R - 24 Moves

B' U D' B R' D' F' D R' L F' R2 F R L' U2 R2 U' R' F2 (20)

25.00 moves NR mean

Harry Savage

at The Great British Blind Off 2019

Round 1

1. R' U' F U' B2 F2 R2 B2 D F2 U' B2 F' U' F2 D L R D' U2 B L' D2 R' U' F

(B R L2 U2 B2 D' B') // pseudo 2x2x3

(F2 D' L) // F2L-1

(F D' F') // 13 to 3e4c

Skeleton: F D F' L' D ^ F2 B D & B2 U2 L2 R' B'

& F D2 F' D2 * //17 to 5c

* D2 F U F' D2 F U' F' (4 cancel)

^ R B2 R' F2 R B2 R' F2 (2 cancel)

Solution: F D F' L' D R B2 R' F2 R B2 R' B D F D2 U F' D2 F U' F' B2 U2 L2 R' B' (27)

2. R' U' F L U2 R D2 R2 F2 L2 F2 D2 R2 B' R' D' R2 D B D' L B F U' R' U' F

(L') F' L2 //square

U' L U' L2 U' D' F' R2 (D') //F2L-1

(F R F2 R' F D' F D) //F2L

(D2 F' D L D' F D2 F' D' L' D F) //LL

Solution: F' L2 U' L U' L2 U' D' F' R2 F' D' L D F D2 F' D L' D' F D F' D F' R F2 R' F' D L (31)

3. R' U' F D' L2 B2 R2 B2 U F2 D U F2 R2 F D R2 B L D' B2 R' D' F2 R' U' F

R L' D' L //EO

(R2 F') //domino

U' R2 D' L2 D' L2 D //domino on all axis
